sutta: this is about transference of merit now the brahmin januszoni came to see the exalted 1 paid homage and sat down to 1 side then he said to the exalted 1 master gotama let me tell you we brahmans give charitable gifts we make the shraddha offerings to the dead saying may this gift to my relatives and blood relations who are dead and gone be of prophet may our kinsmen and blood relations who are dead and go on enjoy this offering master gautama does that gift profit our kinsmen and dead and blood relatives dead and gone do they really enjoy that gift i'll stop here for a moment so you you see from here that these brahmins when their relative passes away they want to do some merit some good for their relative so they offer these uh this food and their customers that they burn the food you know they burn whatever they offer they burn and he's not sure this man is not sure that whether his relative actually gets the offering or not so the buddha said well brahman if there be ground for it it does profit them but not if there be no ground here ground you could say condition if there is the condition they will get it and then he asks master gautama what is ground and what is no ground and the buddha said here in brahman a certain 1 takes life takes what is not given in sexual desires is a wrong doer he's a slanderer of bitter speech and idol babbler covetous harmful in thought and wrong in view when body breaks up after death he rises up again in hell there he subsists on food proper to dwellers in hell on that he is grounded this brahman is the no ground standing on which that gift profits him not i'll stop here for a moment

in the ghost realm they are probably waiting for us to to to help them so that is why whenever we do any good deed we should transfer to all beings sometimes if we are going to do a good deed especially for a relative who has recently passed away or for example even if you move into a new house and uh as soon as you before you move into that house or as soon as you move into that house
